strengths: Novel claim-level schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Ablation confirms LLM linker's causal role in abstention and temporal reasoning
Efficiency gains over A-Mem with -62.1% token usage
Explicit abstention mechanism with behavioral difference from Flat RAG
weaknesses: Overall accuracy not statistically superior to Flat RAG (p=0.21)
Key slices (SSU, abstention) underpowered for definitive claims
Regressions on assistant-authored, preference, and multi-session questions
Intent classifier's low recall limits router effectiveness
required_revisions: Address low statistical power in SSU and abstention slices with larger evaluation
Improve assistant-authored claim extraction to reduce F1 failure mode
Enhance intent classifier recall on multi-session and knowledge-update types
Clarify how multi-claim composition could resolve F3 aggregation failures

strengths: Explicit truth-maintenance edges (contradict/supersede) enable active claim filtering
Evidence-bound abstention mechanism with clear failure-mode analysis
Efficiency gains over A-Mem with -62.1% token usage
Ablation isolates linker as the causal driver of gains
weaknesses: Key capability slices (n=9 abstention, n=21 SSU) underpowered for strong claims
Overall accuracy not statistically superior to Flat RAG despite design goals
Regressions on SSA/SSP/MS slices not fully mitigated by current routers
Intent classifier's low recall on MS/KU limits router effectiveness
required_revisions: Increase abstention/SSU slice sizes to achieve McNemar power (n≥15 for p<0.05 two-sided)
Address F1-F3 failure modes through writer densification or multi-claim composition
Improve router's MS/KU recall via better intent classification or hybrid retrieval
Clarify whether A-Mem reimplementation matches original paper's performance

strengths: Explicit truth-maintenance edges (contradict/supersede) enable active/inactive state tracking
Session-batched writing reduces LLM call overhead vs per-turn systems
Hybrid retrieval (claims + raw turns) preserves RAG's general recall while adding temporal consistency
Statistically significant 100% accuracy on single-session-user slice (n=21) vs Flat RAG's 90.5%
Abstention mechanism explicitly flags 80% of abstention questions without downstream LLM intervention
weaknesses: Overall accuracy statistically indistinguishable from Flat RAG (61.3% vs 68.0%, p=0.21)
Regressions on assistant-authored (SSA), preference (SSP), and multi-session (MS) slices
Ablation shows only 2.6pp drop when removing LLM linker, suggesting limited margin
Intent classifier for TTMG+R-LLM has low recall on MS (42.5%) and KU (39.1%) slices
No cross-encoder reranking despite graph-based retrieval
required_revisions: Address structural limitations in assistant-authored claim extraction (F1 in analysis)
Improve multi-claim composition for MS questions (F3 in analysis)
Increase statistical power for SSU (n=21) and abstention (n=9) slice comparisons
Clarify how TTMG's validity intervals interact with non-temporal questions
Evaluate cross-encoder reranking's impact on consistency filtering

strengths: Novel claim-level schema with explicit validity intervals, provenance, polarity and volatility
Light-weight LLM linker that writes contradict/supersede edges at write time
Greedy maximum-consistent-subgraph retrieval with evidence-bound abstention
Strong empirical ablation showing the linker is causal for every measured slice
Lower token cost (-62%) and latency (-27%) versus A-Mem baseline
Open-source release of code, prompts, splits and per-question outputs
weaknesses: Overall accuracy on the primary 150-Q stratified set is non-significantly *lower* than Flat RAG (61.3% vs 68.0%, p=0.21)
Statistically under-powered slices: abstention n=9 (p=0.125 two-sided), SSU n=21 (p=0.50); full abstention set shows no accuracy gain
Router compositions still below Flat RAG mean and far below oracle (82%); intent classifier recall on MS/KU only 42.5%/39.1%
Writer under-extracts assistant-authored claims (7/7 SSA errors) and multi-claim aggregations (8/8 MS errors)
Evaluation limited to one benchmark (LongMemEval-S) and one reader model (DeepSeek-V3.2); no cross-benchmark or human-reader test
No error bars or significance tests on token/latency reductions; efficiency claims rely on single-run counters
required_revisions: Run power analysis and enlarge evaluation to at least n≈200 per slice to achieve McNemar significance (currently b–c≥6 required)
Add cross-benchmark transfer: test on LoCoMo or MemoryAgentBench to show generality beyond LongMemEval-S
Report confidence intervals and p-values for efficiency metrics (tokens & latency) rather than single-point counts
Ablation study disables only contradict/supersede edges; add ablation that removes *all* claim structure (i.e. revert to flat retrieval) to quantify schema value separately from linker value
Include human evaluation or second reader model to verify that DeepSeek-V3.2 judge bias does not favour TTMG abstention phrasing
Clarify why overall accuracy regression (–6.7 pp) is acceptable for a production system; discuss user-facing trade-offs

strengths: Novel claim-level schema with explicit validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Strong abstention behaviour (80 % explicit vs 0 % baseline) while maintaining equal outcome accuracy
62 % token reduction vs A-Mem with lower latency
Clean ablation shows LLM linker is causal driver of gains
Full reproducibility package: code, prompts, seeds, per-question outputs released
weaknesses: Overall accuracy statistically ties Flat RAG (61.3 % vs 68.0 %, p=0.21) – no significant win
Severely under-powered slices: n=9 abstention (p=0.125) and n=21 SSU (p=0.50) lack significance
Router still loses 12–15 pp to oracle, showing large headroom
regressions on SSA/SSP/MS slices not addressed
No cross-encoder reranker, no BM25 baseline for TTMG, no error-bar on token counts
required_revisions: Run power analysis and enlarge stratified set until McNemar reaches p<0.05 on key slices
Add BM25-only and cross-encoder reranker ablations to justify hybrid design choices
Report standard errors or bootstrap CIs on per-question token counts and latency
Provide confusion matrix for intent classifier to quantify router failure modes
Discuss why overall accuracy drops despite slice-wise wins—attribute to regression slices

strengths: Claim-level schema with validity intervals and explicit contradict/supersede edges is a clean abstraction for truth maintenance
Session-batched writer + lexical gate keeps write-time cost low (-62 % tokens vs A-Mem)
Single-session-user slice shows strict dominance (100 % vs 90.5 %) and abstention behaviour is interpretable
Reproducible: full prompts, code, seeds, per-question logs released
weaknesses: Overall accuracy statistically ties Flat RAG (61.3 % vs 68 %, p=0.21) while regressing on 3/6 slices
Router relies on a weak zero-shot intent classifier (64.7 % acc, 42.5 % MS recall) leaving 12–15 pp on the table
Abstention slice win evaporates on full 30-q set (both 76.7 %); small-signal result
System still needs two retrieval indices (claims + raw turns) and a router, increasing integration surface
No throughput numbers under concurrent load or memory-size scaling beyond 3 sessions
required_revisions: Report per-question latency distribution (p50/p95/p99) and ingestion throughput (claims/sec)
Add memory-size ablation: accuracy vs #claims and vs #sessions to bound index growth
Provide cost model: $/1k tokens for each component and end-to-end $/question vs baseline
Clarify failure probability of lexical gate: % of linker calls that flip-label due to token overlap misses
Include a micro-benchmark of the greedy MCS step under higher hard-edge density (Δ>2)

strengths: Novel application of classical truth maintenance ideas to LLM agent memory with claim-level schema and explicit contradict/supersede edges
Well-designed experiments with proper baselines, statistical tests (McNemar, bootstrap CIs), and informative ablation study
Honest and transparent reporting of mixed results, including detailed failure mode analysis and regression taxonomy
Efficiency gains demonstrated (-62.1% tokens vs A-Mem) with practical deployment considerations
Clear method description with algorithm pseudocode, propositions, and comprehensive appendix with prompts/hyperparameters
weaknesses: Overall accuracy (61.3%) is numerically lower than Flat RAG baseline (68.0%), even if not statistically significant (p=0.21)
Claimed wins on SSU (n=21) and abstention (n=9) slices are underpowered for statistical significance; full abstention set (n=30) shows no accuracy difference
Router composition is needed to recover overall performance, suggesting standalone TTMG is not sufficient
Abstract contains confusing notation (e.g., '58.7 pp→-2.6 pp') that doesn't match table presentation
The explicit abstention behavior (80% vs 0%) is interesting but does not translate to accuracy gains on the full abstention benchmark
required_revisions: Clarify the abstract notation for ablation results ('58.7 pp→-2.6 pp' is confusing; state clearly as 'drops from 61.3% to 58.7%, a -2.6 pp change')
Report effect sizes and confidence intervals for the slice-level comparisons, not just p-values, to help readers assess practical significance
Consider expanding evaluation beyond n=150 stratified slice to increase statistical power for slice-level claims
Add discussion of when/whether explicit abstention is actually beneficial in deployed systems, given the accuracy parity on full abstention set

strengths: Well-motivated problem: truth maintenance for long-horizon agent memory is under-studied
Proper statistical methodology: paired McNemar tests, bootstrap CIs, exact p-values reported
Honest reporting: explicitly acknowledges underpowered tests (n=9 abstention, n=21 SSU) and regressions
Meaningful ablation: removing LLM linker drops all slices, confirming causal role
Detailed failure mode analysis: F1/F2/F3 taxonomy explains regressions structurally
Efficiency gains documented: -62.1% tokens vs A-Mem with latency reduction
Reproducibility artifacts: prompts, hyperparameters, seeds, model identifiers all specified
weaknesses: Underpowered key experiments: abstention (n=9) and SSU (n=21) slices lack statistical significance
Overall regression: TTMG 61.3% vs Flat RAG 68.0% (-6.7pp), even if not statistically significant
Router composition loses abstention benefit: router ABS accuracy equals Flat's 44.4%, not TTMG's 88.9%
Incomplete A-Mem baseline: missing SSA/SSP values in Table 1 (-- entries)
Limited ablation coverage: only --contradict ablation; no ablation of claim schema, temporal parser, or MCS retriever
Weak intent classifier: 64.7% overall, 42.5% MS recall limits router effectiveness
No comparison to classical TMS or temporal KG systems despite related work discussion
required_revisions: Fill missing A-Mem baseline values (SSA/SSP columns) or explain why they cannot be computed
Add ablations isolating claim schema vs linker vs retriever contributions separately
Report standard errors or CIs on per-slice metrics, not just overall accuracy
Clarify why router does not preserve abstention benefit and propose concrete fix
Consider larger sample size for abstention and SSU slices to achieve statistical power
Add comparison to at least one temporal knowledge graph or belief revision baseline

strengths: Novel claim-level memory schema with explicit truth maintenance relations (contradict/supersede edges)
Strong efficiency gains: -62.1% token cost vs A-Mem (13603 vs 35904), lower latency (85s vs 117.1s)
Thorough ablation study demonstrating linker's causal role across all measured slices
Honest statistical reporting with McNemar tests, bootstrap CIs, and explicit power analysis
Clear regression taxonomy (F1-F3) explaining failure modes on SSA/SSP/MS slices
Practical deployment path via inference-time router composition (TTMG-Abstain, TTMG+R-LLM)
weaknesses: Overall accuracy numerically lower than Flat RAG baseline (61.3% vs 68.0%), only statistical parity claimed
Key capability wins on underpowered small slices (n=9 abstention, n=21 SSU) with McNemar p=0.125 and p=0.50
Full 30-question abstention set shows no accuracy difference (both 76.7%), only behavioral difference in abstention rate
Significant regressions on SSA (-35.3pp), SSP (-33.4pp), MS (-7.5pp) require router fallback to recover
Multiple LLM components (writer, linker, parser, reader, intent classifier) increase integration surface and failure points
Intent classifier has low recall on multi-session (42.5%) and knowledge-update (39.1%), limiting router effectiveness
required_revisions: Report effect sizes with confidence intervals for slice-level comparisons, not just p-values and paired counts
Evaluate on larger slices or full benchmark (N=500) to achieve adequate statistical power for claimed improvements
Add write-time latency breakdown (writer vs linker vs indexing) beyond aggregate token counts
Quantify memory/storage overhead of claim graph structure vs flat embedding index
Analyze sensitivity of linker gate thresholds (0.65/0.85) on precision/recall of edge detection
Discuss operational complexity of maintaining dual systems (TTMG + Flat RAG) in production deployment
